Merging ADNI Data

Author

Siena Avolio

Merging ADNI Data

library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
somascanraw <- read_excel("CruchagaLab_CSF_SOMAscan7k_Protein_matrix_postQC_20230620.xlsx")
f01_duke_uplcraw <- read_excel("f01_duke_uplc.xlsx")

lifestyle_dictionaryraw <- read_excel("lifestyle_dictionary.xlsx")
f01_duke_uplc <- f01_duke_uplcraw |>
  select(RID, DHA, EPA, HCys) 

f02a_leiden_hph <- f02a_leiden_hphraw |>
  select(RID, BSH_FA20.5_w3, BSH_FA22.6_w3) 

f02b_leiden_lph <- f02b_leiden_lphraw |>
  select(RID,  BSL_FA20.5_w3, BSL_FA22.6_w3) 

f03_duke_metabolon <- f03_duke_metabolonraw |>
  select(RID, X424, X439, X100008930, X2050, X100000665, X100001181, X848, X100002717, X100002719, X100004494, EXAMDATE) 

f04_npi <- f04_npiraw |>
  select(RID, NPIK, NPIKTOT, NPIK1, NPIK2, NPIK3, NPIK4, NPIK5, NPIK6, NPIK7, NPIK8, NPIK9A, NPIK9B, NPIK9C) 

f05_npiq <- f05_npiqraw |>
  select(RID, NPIK, NPIKSEV)

f06_medhist <- f06_medhistraw |>
  select(RID, MH14ALCH, MH14AALCH, MH16SMOK,
         MH16ASMOK, MH16BSMOK, MH16CSMOK)

f07_bhr_baseline <- f07_bhr_baselineraw |>
  select(RID, QID46_9)

#f08_bhr_longitudinal <- f08_bhr_longitudinalraw |>
  #select(RID, QID50_9, CollectedDate_DRVD)

f09_bhr_caregiver <- f09_bhr_caregiverraw |>
  select(StudyPartnerID, QID44_1_3, QID44_2_3, CollectedDate_DRVD) |>
  mutate(CollectedDate_DRVD = as.Date(CollectedDate_DRVD)) |>
  arrange(StudyPartnerID, CollectedDate_DRVD) |>
  group_by(StudyPartnerID) |>
  slice_head(n = 1) |>
  ungroup()
  
  
#patient 3265 has 3 visits;

 #Study Partner ID is the same as RID, larger numbers because later test

f10_ptdemog <- f10_ptdemograw |>
  select(RID, PTWORK, PTWORKHS)

#socialtest <- f07_bhr_baseline |>
  #full_join(f08_bhr_longitudinal,
           # by = "RID") 
  
#socialtestIDcount <- socialtest |>
  #mutate("n" = count(RID)) |>
  #filter(n > 1)

#take first and last observation for longitudinal data 
datasets <- list(
  f01_duke_uplc,
  f02a_leiden_hph,
  f02b_leiden_lph,
  f03_duke_metabolon,
  f04_npi,
  #f05_npiq,
  f06_medhist,
  f07_bhr_baseline,
  #f08_bhr_longitudinal,
  f10_ptdemog
)

# Convert all RID variables to the same type
datasets <- lapply(datasets, function(x) {
  x |>
    mutate(RID = as.double(RID))
})
Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `RID = as.double(RID)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `RID = as.double(RID)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
# Merge all datasets by RID

lifestyle <- reduce(datasets, full_join, by = "RID")

#Add in 09 dataset

lifestyle <- lifestyle |>
  full_join(f09_bhr_caregiver, 
    by = c("RID" = "StudyPartnerID"))
